project_scope.goal: Build a React frontend and Node.js/TypeScript backend with session-isolated shared PostgreSQL storage, real-time Socket.io progress emissions, and deployment hooks for Vercel and Railway.
constraints: Enforce data isolation with either RLS or mandatory project_id filtering on every JSONB-backed table
Emit Socket.io status events at each micro-step
Deploy frontend to Vercel and backend to Railway

atomic_queue.action: Finalize requirements and generate the project manifest only
Generate exactly one file per iteration
After each file write, run reviewer checks and TypeScript sandbox validation
If build or validation fails, ingest raw logs, identify the exact failure line, and reprompt the coder with that specific error
atomic_queue.rule: Do not continue until the root cause is fixed

atomic_queue.action: Deploy frontend to Vercel and backend to Railway after local build success

database_schema.guardrail: Every row must include project_id and every query must filter by project_id; prefer RLS where supported.

tables.name: projects
columns: created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()

tables.name: project_tasks
columns: project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()

tables.name: project_assets
columns: project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()

tables.name: project_events
columns: project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()

security_rules: Enable RLS on all project-scoped tables
Policy must restrict access to current project_id/session_id context
All backend repository functions must accept project_id explicitly
All JSONB reads/writes must be bounded by project_id
